name: Chad
avoid: Safety advisory: only attempt this itinerary in the November to March dry season and keep checking same-week government advisories, especially for the Lake Chad basin and remote northeastern routes.
A 4x4 with a vetted driver or operator is essential for Zakouma, Ennedi, and most Lake Chad movements; self-drive desert travel is a bad idea without fuel caches, satcom, and local support.
Cards are unreliable outside top N'Djamena hotels, so carry enough XAF cash for park contributions, boat hire, and roadside fuel or food stops.
stopoverNote: Use N'Djamena as a logistics base for cash, permits, charter confirmations, and guide checks, then treat Zakouma, Ennedi, and Lake Chad as pre-arranged legs rather than a spontaneous overland loop.

cities.name: N'Djamena
cities.notes: Capital gateway for visas, cash, and outfitter meetings, with the National Museum of Chad, Grand Marché, and Gaoui Village as the most practical sightseeing anchors.

cities.name: Zakouma National Park
cities.notes: African Parks-run safari destination best accessed by charter or long 4x4 transfer, with Tinga Camp and seasonal Camp Nomade as the main known bases.

cities.name: Ennedi Plateau
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed Sahara landscape of giant arches, canyon camps, rock art, and Guelta d'Archei, usually reached on a guided 4x4 expedition via Fada.

cities.name: Lake Chad
cities.notes: The Chadian side is usually experienced from Baga Sola with short pirogue rides, fish-market stops, and birding when local security conditions are calm enough.

itinerary.sim: Airtel Chad and Moov Africa Chad are the easiest SIMs to buy in N'Djamena; budget roughly ₹700–₹1,700 for a starter data top-up, but expect patchy or zero service once you leave major towns, so download offline maps and rely on guide radios or satphones in Zakouma and Ennedi.

activities.name: Airport taxi into the city centre or riverside hotel zone

Chari River sunset walk or short corniche boat ride

days.theme: Gaoui pottery village and expedition prep day for permits, cash, and 4x4 logistics

activities.name: Half-day Gaoui Village excursion with driver
Pottery workshop tip or local guide at Gaoui
4x4 rental confirmation for remote legs

days.theme: Get into the park and use the first afternoon for a classic Zakouma game drive

activities.name: Shared charter seat from N'Djamena or operator-arranged long 4x4 transfer

days.theme: Full wildlife day for elephants, antelope, and serious dry-season birding

activities.name: Morning safari vehicle with guide if booked à la carte
Afternoon waterhole or floodplain drive
Short ranger-led birding or bush walk where allowed
Laundry or extra camp services

days.theme: Final dawn drive and buffer time before the next remote transfer

activities.name: Last sunrise safari drive
Extra conservation or community contribution at camp
Road or charter exit toward N'Djamena connection
Packed lunch and en-route fuel / checkpoint cash

days.theme: Optional extra safari day if you want a slower, photography-first Zakouma stay

activities.name: Private vehicle supplement for a dedicated photo-focused drive
Additional park day charge where not bundled
Guided hide session at first or last light

days.theme: Reach Fada, settle into camp, and start understanding how remote Ennedi really is

activities.name: Shared charter or domestic/expedition flight from N'Djamena to Fada sector
4x4 expedition share from Fada airstrip to first camp
Ennedi reserve permit and operator handling fee
Camp setup and drinking-water logistics contribution

days.theme: Arch day for Aloba and Bachikélé, the signature sandstone monuments of Chad

activities.name: Shared 4x4 run to Aloba Arch and Bachikélé viewpoints
Ennedi rock-formation sector permit and local guide contribution
Short canyon hike between arch viewpoints
Sunset photography stop at the dunes or rock towers

days.theme: Guelta d'Archei day for crocodiles, camel caravans, and one of the Sahara’s strangest waterholes

activities.name: 4x4 transfer to Guelta d'Archei trailhead
Guelta d'Archei site access, guide, and local camel-herder contribution
Hike into the guelta with pack water and spotter support
Rock-art shelter stop on the return drive

days.theme: Terkei rock art, oasis camps, and a buffer day for desert weather or long drives

activities.name: Terkei or nearby rock-art shelter excursion by 4x4
Guide contribution for archaeological or rock-art stops
Extra fuel and support-vehicle share for longer loops
Return toward Fada or onward expedition camp move

days.theme: Baga Sola transfer, fishing-port atmosphere, and the first dry-season boat ride

activities.name: Return through N'Djamena and continue to Baga Sola by bush taxi or hired 4x4
Lake Chad pirogue or motor-boat trip through reed channels
Baga Sola Lakeside Fishing Port and Market walk
Local guide or fixer for port access and translation

days.theme: Birding, village stops, and an early return if security conditions tighten

activities.name: Early-morning boat for birdlife and fishing-village views
Fresh fish lunch and market purchases
Same-day hired 4x4 or bush taxi back toward N'Djamena
Police checkpoint copies, snacks, and water buffer

connections.method: Most travelers either book a shared charter or commit to a long 4x4 transfer via Mongo or Am Timan; the road works, but only with daylight driving and a serious fuel plan.
Do not try to improvise this as one direct overland jump; the sane version is back through N'Djamena, then onward by charter or expedition flight to Fada and 4x4 into Ennedi.
This leg is usually split via N'Djamena as well, with a flight or expedition exit from Fada, then a hired 4x4 or bush taxi north-west toward Baga Sola once security checks are complete.
Bush taxis are the cheapest way back from the Baga Sola side, but a hired 4x4 is more realistic if you want daylight arrival and less dependence on filling seats.

extras: Plan Chad as an expedition, not a casual hop: remote sectors run on charters, trusted 4x4 crews, and the operator's ability to confirm fuel, water, and the latest local permissions.
Zakouma is the easiest remote leg to book because Tinga Camp and African Parks already structure access; Ennedi and Lake Chad need even more lead time and stronger local security judgement.
Lake Chad can be the first piece you cut if advisories worsen, while Zakouma and guided Ennedi departures are usually the most controlled tourism environments in the country.
Bring printed passport copies, yellow-fever proof, and more cash than you think you need, because road checkpoints, guide tips, and extra fuel often have to be settled in person.
November to March is the usable sweet spot: cooler nights, drier tracks, better wildlife concentrations, and fewer chances of heat or road washouts wrecking a tight itinerary.

Lake Chad: Baga Sola Lakeside Fishing Port and Market
